annotations.label: Signal processing

anchor.snippet: Signal processing is an electrical engineering subfield that focuses on analyzing, modifying and synthesizing signals

annotations.note: Signal processing is an engineering discipline, not a mathematical object formalizable in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Signal as a function of time

anchor.snippet: a signal is represented as a function of time

annotations.note: A signal as a real-valued function of time is not a distinguished Mathlib concept (it is just a function).

annotations.label: Deterministic signal

anchor.snippet: deterministic (then one speaks of a deterministic signal)

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion labeling a function as a deterministic signal.

annotations.label: Stochastic signal as path of a stochastic process

anchor.snippet: a realization of a stochastic process

annotations.note: Mathlib has stochastic process infrastructure (filtrations, stopping, Kolmogorov) but no explicit `stochasticProcess`/signal-realization definition.

annotations.label: Analog signal processing

anchor.snippet: Analog signal processing is for signals that have not been digitized

annotations.note: Analog signal processing is an engineering technology outside Mathlib's scope.

annotations.label: Continuous-time signal processing

anchor.snippet: Continuous-time signal processing is for signals that vary continuously in time

annotations.note: Continuous-time signal processing is an engineering discipline, not a Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: LTI filter output via convolution

anchor.snippet: passing through a linear time-invariant filter/system

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `convolution` and many properties, but does not package the LTI-system/impulse-response equation as a distinguished result.

annotations.label: Impulse response of the system

anchor.snippet: is referred to as the impulse response of the system

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of an impulse response for an LTI system.

annotations.label: Convolution of input and system

anchor.snippet: The above convolution operation is conducted between the input and the system

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `convolution f g L` in `Mathlib.Analysis.Convolution` for functions on an additive group, generalizing the signal-processing convolution.

annotations.label: Discrete-time signal processing

anchor.snippet: Discrete-time signal processing is for sampled signals

annotations.note: Discrete-time signal processing is an engineering discipline, not a Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: Analog discrete-time signal processing

anchor.snippet: Analog discrete-time signal processing is a technology based on electronic devices

annotations.note: This describes analog hardware technology, outside Mathlib's scope.

annotations.label: Discrete-time signal processing as theoretical discipline

anchor.snippet: also refers to a theoretical discipline that establishes a mathematical basis for digital signal processing

annotations.note: No Mathlib definition captures this meta-level discipline.

annotations.label: Digital signal processing

anchor.snippet: Digital signal processing is the processing of digitized discrete-time sampled signals

annotations.note: Digital signal processing is an engineering discipline, not a Mathlib concept.

annotations.label: Nonlinear signal processing

anchor.snippet: Nonlinear signal processing involves the analysis and processing of signals produced from nonlinear systems

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define nonlinear signal processing as such.

annotations.label: Polynomial signal processing

anchor.snippet: Polynomial signal processing is a type of non-linear signal processing

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of polynomial signal processing.

annotations.label: Statistical signal processing

anchor.snippet: Statistical signal processing is an approach which treats signals as stochastic processes

annotations.note: Mathlib has probability infrastructure but no notion labeled statistical signal processing.

annotations.label: Noise model for image denoising

anchor.snippet: one can model the probability distribution of noise incurred when photographing an image

annotations.note: Image noise modeling is not a Mathlib formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Graph signal processing

anchor.snippet: Graph signal processing generalizes signal processing tasks to signals living on non-Euclidean domains

annotations.note: Mathlib has graph theory but no graph signal processing definition.
